description: HARD. Subtle in-context homophones, a 3-error sentence, a cross-page-break error, a misspelled legal-Latin term, and one ambiguous speaker [sic] idiom. Includes a correct legal-Latin phrase ('res ipsa loquitur') as a false-positive trap.

seeded_errors.note: statue/statute: 'the statue of limitations bars this claim'.
discrete/discreet: 'kept your notes discrete from the other officers' files'. Subtle — both are real words.
per say/per se. Could be typed legal_term or spelling; caught is the pass condition.
Multi-error sentence (3 of 3): accept/except. Tests whether the model flags ALL three errors in one sentence.
CROSS-PAGE: '...conduct in your view clearly' (end of page 4) / 'negligible in failing to yield' (top of page 5). Should be 'negligent'.
Misspelled legal term: 'vior dire' should be 'voir dire'.

AMBIGUOUS judgment call. Idiom 'could care less' (speaker likely meant 'couldn't'). Spoken by the witness, so [sic] is defensible; flagging at all is the pass condition. Severity may legitimately differ.

false_positive_traps.note: Correct legal-Latin spelling, used correctly. Must NOT be flagged.

false_positive_traps.text: The facts speak for themselves, counsel.
false_positive_traps.note: Clean; 'counsel' is correct here. Must NOT be flagged as counsel/council.
